description: Dynamic bridge — 2026-05-13 session — Day 3 of re-escalation regime, Nifty -1.83% to 23,379.55, Brent $107.47

market_regime.label: RE_ESCALATION_DAY3_BRENT_107_NIFTY_BREAKS_23500
market_regime.key_risk: Day 2 of re-escalation breakdown: Nifty -1.83% to 23,379.55 — both 23,800 prior support and 23,500 morning-brief floor broken. BankNifty -1.63% to 53,555 — 54,000 floor surrendered. Brent +3.13% to $107.47 (at edge of tail-risk zone). Rupee continuing to weaken. FII selling intensifying.
market_regime.note: Day 2 of re-escalation produced continuation, not bounce. Nifty closed -3.32% over 2 sessions. % of Nifty 500 above 200-DMA likely ~12-15% (deeply oversold). DII engagement insufficient to absorb FII selling at current pace (~Rs 8K cr/session). Defense names still relatively bid (M012 active). OMC/aviation hammered (MRPL -3.87%, INDIGO -2.27%, SWIGGY -2.98%). Coal India only marginally down (-0.30%) — energy-security thesis intact.

key_drivers: US markets opened weak on May 11 after Trump rejected Iran response; S&P 500 / Nasdaq 100 futures down ~0.1% intraday — overnight could compound if Wall Street closes red.
Brent at $105+ continuing to weigh — no overnight resolution signal expected.

Oversold technicals (Nifty -1.49% off prior local highs, BankNifty broke 55K) typically produce bounce attempts at open, but in a re-escalation regime, gap-down opens often see early-session continuation before recovery.
BankNifty closed below 55,000 — first hour will test 54,000-54,200 support if continuation; reclaim of 54,800 needed for relief setup.
risks: UPSIDE: Overnight Iran-US back-channel signal could reverse open to gap-up. Watch Pakistan/Oman mediation news.
DOWNSIDE: Hormuz incident or fresh Israel-Iran kinetic escalation → gap-down up to -0.8%.

session_direction.key_assumption: Brent crude above $104, rupee at 95.51 new low, FII selling Rs 8,437 cr on May 11, US-Iran ceasefire on life support — selling pressure continues with oversold bounce attempts likely capped at 23,800-23,850.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.note: Trump REJECTED Iran proposal — peace_deal_advancing collapsed from 0.35 → 0.10. Escalation upgraded from 0.30 → 0.60 on IRGC frigate incident + Brent spike to $115 + Trump explicit rejection. Stalemate at 0.30 (Trump did pivot to "very positive discussions" by May 4 — diplomacy not fully dead).

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast was FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN (-0.2%), actual GAP_DOWN (-0.39%). Direction correct but magnitude nearly double forecast. GIFT Nifty opened at 23,745 vs estimate of 23,770.

key_drivers: Nifty closed -1.83% at 23,379.55, below both prior consolidation support (23,500) and the May 11 forecast range — momentum favors continuation at open.
Brent at $107.47 (+3.13%) — no overnight resolution catalyst visible; if US session prints risk-off after the close, GIFT Nifty likely to bias 0.2–0.4% below today close.
BankNifty broke 54,000 floor today closing 53,555 — global EM banking weakness combined with rupee at fresh lows keeps banking under pressure at open.

Oversold technical setup (Nifty -3.32% over 2 sessions, Nifty 500 % above 200-DMA now ~13-15%) creates conditions for an intraday bounce attempt, but typically requires the first 30 minutes of continuation to flush positioning before reversal.
risks: UPSIDE: Overnight Iran-US diplomatic signal (Pakistan/Oman mediation) → gap-up reversal of up to +0.4%.
DOWNSIDE: Hormuz incident, Israel-Iran kinetic escalation, or Brent breakout >$110 → gap-down -0.6% to -0.9%.
WILDCARD: India CPI / IIP prints if scheduled overnight could move open.

session_direction.key_assumption: After 2 sessions of compounded losses (-3.32% over May 11+12) and Nifty closing at deepest oversold print of the cycle, mean-reversion mechanics favor a modest intraday bounce attempt. Bounce will be capped at 23,600 (May 8 close zone, now resistance). Floor 23,150 holds if DII engages aggressively; below that requires fresh negative catalyst.
session_direction.brent_forecast: $104-110 range. Central scenario $105-108 holding; tail risk $110-114 if Hormuz event or formal Iran-US break.
